quality.rationale: 基于四极矩辐射缩放律的多步量纲分析，关联宏观爆炸参数与微观引力子数，物理逻辑连贯且具备天体物理背景

origin.question: Problem 18.16. Estimate the number of gravitons emitted in an asymmetric explosion of energy E.
origin.solution: Solution 18.16. The characteristic quadrupole gravitational-wave power of a physical process is $(\mathrm{G} / \mathrm{c}^{5})|\ddot{\mathrm{Q}}|^{2}\sim (\mathrm{G} / \mathrm{c}^{5})\mathrm{M}^{2}\mathrm{v}^{4} / \mathrm{T}^{2}$ where M, v, T are relevant masses, velocities and times for the process. This can be conveniently written as$$\mathrm {P}_{\mathrm {G W}} \sim \frac {\mathrm {G}}{\mathrm {c}^{5}} \left(\frac {\mathrm {M v}^{2}}{\mathrm {T}}\right)^{2} \sim \frac {\mathrm {P}^{2}}{3 \times 1 0^{5 9} \mathrm {e r g s / s e c .}}.$$Here $\mathrm{P} \sim \mathrm{Mv}^2 / \mathrm{T}$ represents the characteristic "power" of internal energy flows.For an explosion of energy E, characteristic time $\pmb{\tau}$ the "internal power flow" is $\mathbf{E} / \tau$ . If we assume that this internal power flow couples efficiently to gravitational waves, then in $c = G = 1$ units$$\mathrm {P}_{\mathrm {G W}} \sim (\mathrm {E} / \tau)^{2}$$and$$\mathrm {E}_{\mathrm {G W}} \sim \mathrm {P}_{\mathrm {G W}} \tau \sim \mathrm {E}^{2} / \tau .$$The typical graviton emerging from this explosion will have energy $\hbar \omega \sim \hbar / \tau$ so the number of gravitons emerging will be of order$$N \sim \frac {E^{2} / \tau}{\hbar / \tau} = \frac {E^{2}}{\hbar} \sim \left(\frac {E}{1 0^{1 6} \mathrm {e r g s}}\right)^{2}.$$
